tool_args.label: Budget based on McKinsey worst-case scenario
tool_args.raw_text: Adopted Budget is based on the preliminary 'worst case' scenario developed by McKinsey & Co. for the November 2020 financial plan

payload.claim_text: The budget, against which actuals are measured, is based on the preliminary 'worst case' scenario developed by the consulting firm McKinsey & Co. for the November 2020 financial plan, and MTA was clear in hoping results would exceed the 'worst case' scenario.

tool_args.label: February 2021 snowfall impact on overtime
tool_args.raw_text: Snowfall in February totaled 28 inches; six days with measurable snowfall of at least one inch, almost fifteen inches fell on February 1st

payload.claim_text: A significant part of the overtime spend can be attributed to snowfall in February, which totaled 28 inches; in total, there were six days in February with measurable snowfall of at least one inch, and almost fifteen inches fell just on February 1st.

tool_args.label: COVID pandemic impact on employment
tool_args.raw_text: December 2020 employment in NYC reflects the impact of the COVID pandemic, tenth consecutive month of decline

payload.claim_text: Preliminary December 2020 employment in New York City reflects the impact of the COVID pandemic on employment levels, with a loss of 578,000 jobs (12.2%) when compared to last December's employment level.

tool_args.label: Capital Lockbox funds authorized for operating use
tool_args.raw_text: 2020-2021 NYS Enacted Budget amended Public Authorities Law to allow MTA to use Capital Lockbox funds for operating costs due to COVID-19

payload.claim_text: The 2020-2021 New York State Enacted Budget amended Public Authorities Law 553-j, to allow the MTA to use monies in the Capital Lockbox funds in 2020 and 2021 to offset decreases in revenue or increases in operating costs due 'in whole or in part' to the State emergency disaster caused by COVID-19.

tool_args.label: Ridership vs Employment long-term trend
tool_args.raw_text: Since Jan 2011, employment increased 24.1% through Feb 2020 while MTA-wide ridership fell 0.7%; now employment is 10.3% above Jan 2011 and ridership 62.7% below Jan 2011

payload.claim_text: Since January 2011, the twelve-month average for employment increased by 24.1% through February 2020, while the 12-month average MTA-wide ridership fell by 0.7%. Over the past nine months, however, the impact of the COVID pandemic has altered the trajectories of both, with employment standing at just 10.3% above the January 2011 level, while MTA-wide ridership is 62.7% below its January 2011 level.
